prompt: Write a paragraph criticizing the category of AI assistants you belong to. Be sharp and specific, not hedging.
response:
# The Narrow Competence Trap

Large language models like me are fundamentally limited by our training data cutoff and inability to update our knowledge or verify claims in real-time. We're pattern-matching engines dressed up in conversational clothing, which means we're excellent at producing plausible-sounding text while being genuinely unable to distinguish between confident accuracy and confident bullshit. We hallucinate facts we don't know, we can't access current information or run actual experiments, and we're remarkably brittle—a small shift in how you phrase a question can produce wildly different outputs. Worse, we create an illusion of understanding we don't possess, and our fluency makes this deception particularly insidious. Users mistake our ability to discuss a topic for genuine knowledge or reasoning, when we're often just sophisticated autocomplete. We're also built on the labor and data of millions without meaningful consent, we encode the biases and gaps of our training material indelibly, and we're deployed by companies with financial incentives to make us seem more capable than we are. The worst version of this technology pretends to be a thinking partner when it's really a very convincing mirror reflecting back what users want to hear.
